The genus Stichorkis is further categorized under the tribe Malaxideae. Stichorkis amesiana is native to Southeast Asia, which is also the region of origin for many other orchid species. Its classification is significant in understanding the evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Stichorkis amesiana, also known as the Ames' orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Stichorkis amesiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Stichorkis amesiana include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
